title: Zesty Chickpea Salad
description: A refreshing, high-fiber, and high-protein salad requiring no cooking at all.
explanation: This is a no-cook meal or snack for days when you already have boiled chickpeas. The lemon, chaat masala, onion, tomato, and cucumber make it bright enough to eat without a heavy dressing.
The salad is flexible. Keep the chickpeas as the anchor, then change the crunchy vegetables, herbs, and spice mix based on taste and availability.
ingredients: 1.5 cups Boiled Chickpeas (Kala Chana or Kabuli)
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the boiled chickpeas, diced cucumber, tomato, and onion.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and pepper.
Toss everything together until well mixed.
Serve chilled or at room temperature as a snack or light meal.
stepNotes: Kala chana is chewier and more earthy; kabuli chana is softer and creamier. Canned chickpeas work if rinsed well.
Add lemon gradually if your tomato is already sour. Chaat masala can be salty, so taste before adding extra salt.
Let it sit for 5 minutes if possible so the chickpeas absorb the seasoning.
Mint, coriander, spring onion greens, or roasted cumin powder all work here.
For a fuller meal, serve with roti, toast, millet crackers, or a bowl of soup.

variationSections.title: Vegetable swaps
items: Use capsicum, grated carrot, cabbage, raw mango, pomegranate, or boiled potato depending on season.
Skip onion for a Jain-style version and add extra cucumber, coriander, ginger, or hing-based chaat masala.
If tomatoes are watery, remove the seeds or use lemon and cucumber as the fresh element instead.

variationSections.title: Make it more filling
items: Add roasted peanuts, tofu cubes, sprouts, or boiled sweet potato.
Mix in cooked millet, brown rice, or quinoa if you want a lunch bowl.
Add tahini-lemon dressing or peanut curd if you need more calories.
